heading: Compulsory retirement at age of sixty-two
label: 2304 Compulsory retirement at age of sixty-two
text: Any enlisted member who has reached the age of sixty-two shall be retired from active service.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 14, U.S.C., 1946 ed., § 173 (, ).Apr. 12, 1902, ch. 501, § 432 Stat. 100, U.S.C., 1946 ed., has been divided. That part dealing with enlisted men is placed in this section. That part dealing with commissioned officers is placed in . That part dealing with warrant officers is placed in .Section 173 of title 14section 230 of this titlesection 303 of this titleThe compulsory retirement age is changed from 64 to 62 in order to make it the same for enlisted men as for officers. 81st Congress, House Report No. 557.

Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282section 353 of this title1986— struck out “, with retired pay of the grade or rating with which retired” after “active service”.Pub. L. 99–3481984— substituted reference to enlisted member for reference to enlisted man.Pub. L. 98–557
